definition: The abstract substrate every dialog-based surface conforms to — a top-layer surface that interrupts or overlays the page, owning the shared modal-surface machinery (focus containment, escape/outside/backdrop dismissal, scroll-lock, modal/non-modal mode, top-layer mount). Not a shippable widget; its concrete members are distinct configurations of the Modal Intent's placement over this one substrate: centered modal (center), drawer (start/end), bottom sheet (bottom). The droplist-family pattern applied to dialogs (droplist : dropdown :: dialog : drawer).
usage: The Dialog block (abstract), realized on native <dialog> + the Popover API; implements the Modal Intent and composes Focus Containment, Surface, and Motion. Concrete members (drawer #360, sheet, modal) extend it rather than re-implementing the machinery.
